canonical_question: What R&D tax credits are available globally — US Section 41, UK relief, French CIR?
aliases: R&D tax credit comparison by country
Research and development tax incentives global overview
Section 41 IRC research credit qualified research expenses
UK RDEC merged scheme R&D relief
constraints: Jurisdiction: each country's domestic legislation defines eligible activities, rates, caps, and documentation; no single global R&D credit exists
Temporal: the US restored immediate expensing of domestic R&D under Section 174A via OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025); UK merged scheme effective April 1, 2024; France CIR rules updated February 15, 2025

Entity threshold: US qualified small business payroll offset ($500K cap) requires <= $5M gross receipts and < 5 years old; Canada SR&ED enhanced rate limited to first CAD 3M for CCPCs; UK ERIS requires 30% R&D intensity
Prerequisite: all major jurisdictions require contemporaneous documentation of qualifying activities before claiming credits; retroactive documentation provides limited protection

Interaction: R&D credits interact with transfer pricing rules for contract R&D, Pillar Two global minimum tax (GloBE ETR calculations include R&D credits), and state/provincial tax incentives that may stack or conflict

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking specifically about US transfer pricing for contract R&D between affiliates
User is asking about Pillar Two global minimum tax and its interaction with tax incentives
User is asking about general corporate tax rates rather than R&D-specific incentives

related_to.label: Transfer pricing rules (interact with contract R&D pricing)

inputs_needed.question: In which country or countries does the company perform R&D and file taxes?
options: United States — Section 41 credit + Section 174A expensing
United Kingdom — merged RDEC scheme or ERIS

inputs_needed.question: What is the company's size and revenue level?
options: Startup or small business (< $5M revenue / SME equivalent)

inputs_needed.question: What type of R&D activities are performed?

applies_to.user_segment: Companies performing R&D activities seeking to reduce tax liability through R&D incentives
applies_to.context: Any entity conducting qualifying research and development, from startups to large multinationals, across major OECD jurisdictions
